findings: bin/stampede-monitor.sh is observer-only: no stop/terminate capability (no signals, no tmux termination, no pidfile usage).
PIDS_DIR is declared in monitor but unused, so monitor cannot reconcile/act on launcher PID tracking.
Agent status mapping is fragile: assumes fleet.json iteration order aligns with tmux pane indices; can misreport.
Header RUNNING/WAITING derives from claimed/queued file counts not tmux liveness; can mislead until orphan recovery triggers.
Orphan recovery only runs when live_agents==0; partial-death scenarios can strand claimed tasks.
No tmux session teardown on monitor exit; no signal traps; minor untracked background afplay job.
